trivia_bullets.text: Only printing; from Modern Horizons 3 (MH3, June 2024), a directly-to-Modern supplemental set that returned the Eldrazi to prominence. MH3's Eldrazi suite reintroduced the Drone subtype and Devoid mechanic — design decisions marking Eldrazi as a distinct production-line faction with its own mechanical grammar.

Type line: Creature — Eldrazi Drone. Eldrazi Drones in MTG lore are the lowest-caste Eldrazi, extensions of the titan will that exist only to process and consume matter. The Repurposer name extends this: its specific job is to recycle what was consumed into mana-generating Spawn tokens.
sources: Scryfall: type_line; Eldrazi lore context from MTG design history

trivia_bullets.text: "I almost admire their ability to avoid waste. Almost." — General Tazri, allied commander. Tazri is the legendary creature anchoring the Allied Commander deck archetype (see Tazri, Beacon of Unity, Zendikar Rising). The 'Almost' preserves the horror register.

EDHREC rank 8,623 — the highest Commander presence of any card in this bundle, reflecting MH3's 2024 release and the card's genuine utility. Pauper-legal at common. Double trigger (Spawn on cast AND on death) generates value in two directions, fitting artifact-sacrifice and colorless-mana strategies. Legal in Historic, Modern, Legacy, Commander, Pauper.
Illustrated by Daren Bader, a MTG artist with decades of work including many creature cards across multiple blocks.
